title: Scroll-driven UI — placement of the continuous scroll-progress driver
summary: Prior-art survey grounding decision #1407 (verb-axis straggler of #1390): UI driven by scroll position/progress — scroll-spy (highlight the active nav section), scroll-linked animation (progress bars, reveals, parallax), sticky-on-scroll. WE owns viewport-presence (enter/leave observation) and animation-orchestration (intra-element build-ins), but neither owns CONTINUOUS scroll-progress as a driver. Native grounding: CSS Scroll-driven Animations Module L1 is the substrate — animation-timeline binds scroll() (progress of a scroll container — the continuous driver for progress bars/parallax/sticky-progress; args scroller nearest|root|self + axis block|inline|x|y) or view() (progress of an element through the viewport; args axis + start/end insets — the per-element reveal driver), with named ranges cover/contain/entry/exit scoped via animation-range, and scroll-timeline-name/view-timeline-name for the non-anonymous form. Progress is a normalized 0..1 value across the chosen range — the single conceptual primitive. Support (June 2026): Chrome/Edge/Opera full, Safari 26 shipped full, Firefox implemented behind a flag; animation-timeline is silently ignored by non-supporting engines so the lowering path is clean (declare intent -> emit native scroll()/view() -> @supports guard -> JS fallback via IntersectionObserver/scroll listener writing a --progress custom property, or WAAPI ScrollTimeline/ViewTimeline, only where absent). prefers-reduced-motion targets exactly this motion class, so the intent composes motion (reduced -> static end-state). Load-bearing finding: continuous scroll-progress is a DISTINCT DRIVER, not an enter/leave boolean — Motion useScroll->scrollYProgress is 'a value between 0 and 1', GSAP ScrollTrigger exposes scrub + progress 0..1 + pin/parallax, CSS scroll()/view() ARE a normalized progress timeline; categorically distinct from viewport-presence's enter/leave (IntersectionObserver answers in/out, NOT a continuous position). The residual recurs across three UX shapes (progress bar, parallax/reveal, sticky-progress). Finding 2: scroll-spy is a DISCRETE active-section concern (Bootstrap ScrollSpy rewritten onto IntersectionObserver; picks which section is current — a discrete winner = viewport-presence enter/leave + a 'pick the most-present' reducer), NOT the continuous driver. Finding 3: sticky/'stuck' state is native and separable (position:sticky + the new scroll-state(stuck:) container query, no JS; Lenis/Locomotive wrap native scroll so sticky keeps working); smooth-scroll/parallax libraries (Lenis/Locomotive/GSAP/AOS) are impls of the same driver -> FUI-side adapters, not WE vocabulary (impl-is-not-a-standard, minimize-lock-in). WE-tree: viewport-presence owns the discrete in/out trigger over IntersectionObserver (reference/margin/coverage; enter/leave; 'owns the trigger, not the UX decision'; NO continuous position); animation-orchestration owns intra-element build-in sequencing (sequence parallel|stagger|chain, trigger on-view|on-step|on-scroll glossed 'scroll-linked progress (Scroll-driven Animations)', replay once|re-enter; composes motion) but consumes on-scroll as an OPAQUE start condition, never modeling the scroll-progress value/range/axis vocabulary; the web-scroll-observation research topic already ruled 'decompose onto native seams', classified 'scroll-driven animations (animation-timeline — a motion concern)', carved out only viewport-presence, and left the motion-side continuous driver UNBUILT — that gap is exactly #1407. Unowned residual: a continuous scroll-progress DRIVER (normalized 0..1 from scroll() or view(), scoped by animation-range over named ranges on an axis). Recommendation: (Fork 1, ~85%) a NEW scroll-progress driver intent (homed webintents), composed by animation-orchestration and others — extending viewport-presence is BROKEN (its charter is the boolean IntersectionObserver trigger, the wrong native substrate for a continuous ScrollTimeline value); folding into animation-orchestration over-couples (scroll-progress drives non-animation consumers too: scroll-spy nav, reading-progress, sticky-progress) so per bias-toward-separation it earns its own composable home and animation-orchestration COMPOSES it (replacing its opaque on-scroll trigger). (Fork 2 sub-fork, ~80%) scroll-spy = a COMPOSITION of viewport-presence (observe N sections) + the consumer choosing the active one, NOT new vocabulary — folding discrete active-section into the continuous driver is broken (mis-models a 'most-present of N' reducer as a 0..1 value); watch item: if 'which section is active' needs shared vocabulary, a thin viewport-presence 'most-present' selection mode could be warranted. Net: #1407 builds ONE new intent (scroll-progress); scroll-spy + sticky stay compositions of existing intents + native CSS. Build note: rewire animation-orchestration.trigger:on-scroll's gloss to cite scroll-progress. Report: reports/2026-06-21-scroll-progress-driver-placement.md.
